sections.overviewSubtitle: You have your devices and DNS rules here. Add a device to get started, then manage block/allow lists below.

sections.blockAllowSubtitle: Block or allow websites for each device. Domains listed here are checked when the device uses our DNS.

dnsPrompt.title: Use our DNS for this device?
dnsPrompt.text: Get setup in one step: use the browser extension (this browser only) or download a setup file (whole device).

logoutConfirm: Are you sure you want to log out?

devices.longIntro: Each device gets a personal DNS link. When a phone, PC, or browser uses that link, lookups go through this service: we apply your block/allow rules (and optional adult blocking), log blocks to your dashboard, then answer or forward the query.
devices.longIntroSecondary: Need help? See setup steps for each device type on the
devices.adultBlockedOn: Adult content blocked for this device
devices.adultAllowed: Adult content allowed for this device
devices.intro: Add a device to get a unique setup link. Then use the extension or download the setup file for your platform.
devices.name: Name
devices.useThisUrl: Use this URL when setting up DNS for this device:
devices.noDevices: No devices yet. Add one to get started.
devices.revokeConfirm: Revoke this device? It will stop using your DNS.

blockAllow.intro: Block or allow websites for each device. Domains listed here are checked when the device uses our DNS.
blockAllow.addDeviceFirst: Add a device first in the Devices section.
blockAllow.noRules: No rules for this device yet.
blockAllow.blockLogIntro: Domains that were blocked when this device used our DNS. Add block rules above to block more.
blockAllow.blockLogEmpty: No blocked requests yet. Once this device uses your DoH URL and hits a blocked domain, it will appear here.

help.heading: Two ways to enable DNS control
help.intro: Use the browser extension (this browser only) or download a setup file for your platform (whole device). After you add a device in the Devices section, you can get the extension link or download the setup file.
help.browserExtensionDesc: Install our extension from the dashboard or store. One click—no system settings. Filtering applies to that browser only (Chrome, Firefox, etc.).
help.setupFileIntro: In Devices, add a device and click Download setup file for your platform:
help.windows: Windows — Download the file and run it once (approve "Run as administrator" if prompted).
help.mac: Mac — Download the file or profile and run / install it once.
help.linux: Linux — Download the script and run it once with sudo.
help.ios: iOS — Download the configuration profile, open it, then go to Settings → Profile Downloaded → Install.
help.android: Android — Download our app (or use the Play Store link) and tap Enable in the app.
help.manualSetupDesc: If the downloaded file does not work on your OS version, you can configure DNS manually: open your system or Wi‑Fi settings, find DNS, and enter the DoH URL shown for your device in the Devices section.

settings.interfaceLanguageHint: Saved to your account and used across the dashboard.

privacyPanel.heading: Privacy & Confidentiality Commitment
privacyPanel.intro: This platform lets you control DNS for your devices: block or allow websites per device. Your account, devices, and block/allow rules are stored so the service can apply your preferences. We do not sell your data.
privacyPanel.dataProtectedDesc: We store only what is needed to run the service: account details (name, email, password hash), devices you add and their secure tokens, and block/allow rules you set. We may optionally log DNS query counts per device for statistics; we do not sell your data.
privacyPanel.noDataCollectionDesc: We don't track, collect, or store your personal information
privacyPanel.completePrivacyDesc: Your data remains private and confidential at all times
privacyPanel.openSourceDesc: This is an open-source project - transparent and trustworthy
privacyPanel.yourControlDesc: You decide which sites to block or allow per device; you can revoke a device at any time
privacyPanel.peaceOfMindDesc: Use DNS control with peace of mind. We keep only the data needed to provide the service and do not sell or share it with third parties for marketing.

helpPanel.heroTitle: Connect your devices to DMS Control
helpPanel.heroLead: You don’t type a classic “DNS number” here. Each device gets a personal secure link (DNS-over-HTTPS). Your browser uses it for lookups: only domains on your block list (or optional adult filter) are stopped; everything else loads as usual. Allow rules undo a block for specific sites.
helpPanel.openDevicesSub: Add a device and copy your link there
helpPanel.step1Body: Add a phone, laptop, or another device. Each one gets its own link and rules.
helpPanel.step2Body: Use Copy next to the long URL—that’s what you paste, not a normal site address for browsing.
helpPanel.step3Title: Paste into Firefox, Chrome, or your system
helpPanel.step3Body: Follow the guides below, or download a setup file from Devices for your whole computer.
helpPanel.dohSectionTitle: Your DNS links (same as on Devices)
helpPanel.dohEmpty: You don’t have a device yet. Add one on the Devices page—then your personal link will show here.
